# Override the command in the file header

The header of every `pip-compile` output reproduces the command that produced it:

```text
#
# This file is autogenerated by pip-compile with Python 3.13
# by the following command:
#
#    pip-compile --output-file=requirements.txt pyproject.toml
#
```

When you wrap `pip-compile` in a script, the header still shows the wrapped command. Override it with the
`CUSTOM_COMPILE_COMMAND` environment variable:

```console
CUSTOM_COMPILE_COMMAND="./scripts/recompile-deps" pip-compile pyproject.toml
```

The header becomes:

```text
#
# This file is autogenerated by pip-compile with Python 3.13
# by the following command:
#
#    ./scripts/recompile-deps
#
```

## When to use it

**A wrapper script encodes the right flags.** Your team has a `scripts/recompile-deps` shell script that
runs `pip-compile` with the project's preferred flags. You want the lockfile header to show the script
name, so contributors know what to run when they regenerate.

**Multiple lockfiles, one wrapper.** A monorepo with several lockfiles uses one wrapper that compiles
all of them. Each lockfile's header should show the wrapper command, not the underlying `pip-compile`
invocation.

**A Make or just target.** A project compiles via `make deps`. Setting `CUSTOM_COMPILE_COMMAND="make
deps"` means the lockfile header points contributors at the Make target instead of `pip-compile` flags.

## What the variable does not change

`CUSTOM_COMPILE_COMMAND` only changes the comment in the header. It does not change:

- The actual command `pip-compile` runs (it still does its normal work).
- Which inputs `pip-compile` reads.
- Where the output gets written.
- Whether the header gets emitted at all (`--no-header` strips the whole block, regardless of the
  variable).

## Strip the header instead

If you want no header at all, `--no-header` is the right flag:

```console
pip-compile --no-header
```

The output starts straight at the first pinned package. Useful when the lockfile gets included in larger
build artifacts that have their own header.

## Combining with `--header`

`--no-header` and `CUSTOM_COMPILE_COMMAND` are mutually exclusive in effect: `--no-header` strips
everything, including the variable's substitution. `--header` (the default) emits the header with either
the original command or the variable's value.

```{seealso}
- {doc}`customize-output` for `--no-header`, `--no-annotate`, and the `--no-emit-*` flags.
```
